metadata.title: 张巍GRE镇考机经词-乱序版 7.0

fields.meaning: GRE常考意思（保留英文释义和中文释义）

words.meaning: adj. offensive to the senses and especially to the sense of smell 令人讨厌的,恶臭的
words.example: Most people deal with overwhelming anger or hurt or humiliation or shame—any of the hot, noisome feelings that form in the gut and move slowly up the throat—by marshalling their strength and batting those thoughts back down.

words.meaning: n. incapable of error 无过失
words.example: The combination of free-form opportunism, heroic self-regard, blithe contempt for expertise, and an airy sense of infallibility has contributed to Trump’s profound estrangement from the truth.

words.meaning: adj. brief and direct in a way that may see rude or unfriendly 简短的
words.example: Lightfoot, speeding on a country road in a stolen muscle car, picks up the fleeing Thunderbolt and outdrives the gunman for kicks—and experiences a sort of fraternal love at first sight for his terse, coolly confident and worldly- wise older passenger.

words.meaning: adj. extremely silly, foolish, or unreasonable 荒谬的
words.example: The local population from Brazil to Mexico lives with a level of state-sponsored and social violence that reaches absurd levels.

words.meaning: (1)n. the act or: process of freeing someone or something from another’s control 解放 (2)n. the removal of traditional social or sexual rules, attitudes, etc. 解脱
words.example: Over six days in late November and early December, 2016, Fatah, the Palestinian national liberation movement, convened its seventh congress in Ramallah, the de-facto capital of the Palestinian Authority.

words.meaning: v. to make no longer necessary 避免,取消
words.example: Unconventional war fits no pattern, defies the military planner's imagination—and might obviate the use of force in the first place.

words.meaning: adj. aggressively malicious 恶性的
words.example: McCain’s surgery revealed a glioblastoma, an aggressively malignant brain tumor.

words.meaning: adj. including or involving many things 彻底的
words.example: It was the pictures themselves that conveyed a sweeping and indelible portrait of American poverty, and continued Richards’s work as a descendant of photographers from Jacob Riis to Walker Evans.

words.meaning: adj. of, relating to, or being the basic or essential constituent of something 基本的
words.example: After escaping the anxiety of influence, the poet discovered a brilliant, elemental poetry.

words.meaning: v. to gradually become smaller 逐渐减少,逐渐变小
words.example: Such people still exist, but their number has dwindled, not least because rising ticket prices have made habitual attendance harder.

words.meaning: adj. opposed to someone or something in a very angry or determined way that cannot be changed 无法安抚的
words.example: Chris’s fierce efforts at self-liberation, and a self-image that’s deeply imbued with the memory of violence, make for a rhapsodic, visionary, yet harshly implacable drama.

words.meaning: (1)adj. easy or possible to get or use 可获得的 (2)adj. present and able or willing to talk to someone 有空的
words.example: We have reviewed your request for a romantic comedy featuring middle-aged/senior actors and have prepared the following list of fifty available titles.

words.meaning: (1)v. to refuse to accept or support 拒绝承认 (2)v. to refuse to having anything to do with 与...断绝关系
words.example: President Trump’s remarks today repudiate his forced remarks yesterday about the KKK and neo-Nazis.

words.meaning: adj. not favoring one side or group over another 公平的
words.example: The pathologies of American journalism are by now clichés: aversion to policy analysis; addiction to horse-race politics; smashing of walls that once separated news, opinion and advertising; an ideology that mistakes evenhandedness for objectivity.

words.meaning: v. to walk or travel over 旅行
words.example: She demonstrates a great extent of peregrination, as she has traveled to many more countries and places around the world than any of her kindred.

words.meaning: v. to increase or improve 提高,改善
words.example: They can be used to create a mood or a feeling for the entire book, be more specific to the text, or just be purely decorative and enhance the appeal of the overall book design.

words.meaning: adj. very surprising, shocking, or frightening 令人惊讶的
words.example: Shortly after an amiable meeting with the Emir of Qatar, President Trump threw him under the diplomatic bus in a series of startling tweets.

words.meaning: (1)n. an artist, performer, or player of consummate skill 能 手 (2)n. one having control 有控制权的人 (3)v. to become skilled or proficient in the use of 精通
words.example: The European master Peter Eötvös’s adaptation of Tony Kushner’s play gets its New York première, at New York City Opera.

words.meaning: n. friendly social atmosphere 友谊
words.example: Comity implies mutual admiration, or at least mutual respect.

words.meaning: v. to say that something is true in a confident way 确信
words.example: He believes that Buddhist doctrine and practice anticipate and affirm the “modular” view of the mind favored by much contemporary cognitive science.

words.meaning: adj. involving or having information that disagrees with other information 矛盾的
words.example: Throughout his career, Trump has deliberately stirred conflict among his underlings, chafed at efforts to rein him in, and reserved the right to act in arbitrary and contradictory ways.

words.meaning: adj. in a state of complete disorder and confusion 混乱的
words.example: The chaotic and deadly events in Charlottesville were followed by a largely peaceful anti-racism march in Boston.

words.meaning: n. a sudden increase in knowledge, understanding, etc. 突 破
words.example: The inaugural breakthrough in the field of hearing restoration occurred in the late nineteen-eighties, when two researchers discovered that the ears of young chickens do something that human ears don’t.

words.meaning: v. to ignore in a deliberate and insulting way 冷落
words.example: The university’s once snubbed department of economic history has lost prestige and transmogrified into a department of management and marketing.

words.meaning: n. something that impedes progress or achievement 障碍
words.example: I wasn’t directed so much as given a series of props to make my way through, like an obstacle course.

words.meaning: v. to make impossible by necessary consequence: rule out in advance 排除,防止
words.example: This had a kind of elegance that seemed to preclude the more brutish ambitions of Everest.

words.meaning: n. slant, bias 倾斜,偏见
words.example: The tilt in Hillary Clinton’s favor among financial-industry types may have less to do with their love of her than with a rejection of the instability that Donald Trump represents.

words.meaning: adj. lasting, durable 持久的
words.example: Lula’s enduring appeal stems in part from the economic boom he oversaw during his term as President, when thirty million people in Brazil were lifted out of extreme poverty.

words.meaning: adj. changing often 善变的
words.example: How did the fickle sands of time slip through your fingers so easily?

words.meaning: (1)v. to limit or restrict 限制 (2)v. to use pressure to force to do something 强迫
words.example: Speaking to a group of reporters recently, Mattis said that he remained critical of the nuclear deal, mainly because it did not constrain Iran’s aggressive activities in the region, but that he considered it binding.

words.meaning: adj. involving two people or two sides who oppose each other 对立的
words.example: They use a grand jury, before which testimony is under oath and transcribed, to lock in testimony from more adversarial witnesses.

words.meaning: adj. requiring much time, attention, or effort from someone 严格的
words.example: Some members of his band have been playing with him since the nineteen-seventies, and they know him as an easygoing but exacting leader who wants his songs to sound just the way fans remember them.

words.meaning: adj. following one after the other in a series 连续的
words.example: Against the complex techniques of postwar Western composition, Riley, a native Californian, posited a piece with a one-page score, entrusting a group of musicians to sing or play fifty-three brief musical fragments in successive, overlapping waves.

words.meaning: adj. pointless or useless 无用的
words.example: It’s self-comforting, rather, in its exercise of Bush’s never-doubted sincerity and humility—virtues that were maddeningly futile when he governed, and that now shine brighter, in contrast with Trump, than may be merited.

words.meaning: (1)v. to become smaller or less 衰落,减少 (2)n. a time of phase of gradual decrease 衰落期
words.example: Lindsay has been busy since early summer releasing graphs and chart that seem to indicate that the narcotics problem in the city was on the wane long before September 1st, when the Rockefeller bill went into effect.

words.meaning: v. to show 表示
words.example: The wooden-house set pieces and multi-hued backdrops that fly gracefully in and out betoken the humble, homespun lives outlined in the stories of Sholom Aleichem, on which the musical is based.

words.meaning: adj. showing kindness and gentleness 和善的
words.example: Most crucially, he never faced the dehumanizing weapons and tactics of modern asymmetric warfare, in which death can arrive, unseen and unanticipated, even from the most seemingly safe or benign of encounters.

words.meaning: n. the quality of seeming real 逼真
words.example: If you come from anything like Angelou’s admired family, you will recognize the verisimilitude of her words, and how Cash fits her body into them.

words.meaning: v. to make ill-humored or discontented 使不高兴
words.example: In this episode of The Cartoon Lounge, with the help of the cartoonist Avi Steinberg, we read the sweetest complaint ever, from a disgruntled six-year-old girl who wants to know why kids can’t enter the caption contest.

words.meaning: n. the quality or state of having many different forms, types, ideas, etc. 多样性,差异化
words.example: Last week, Google fired a software engineer for writing a memo that questioned the company’s gender diversity policies and made statements about women’s biological suitability for technical jobs.

words.meaning: adj. tending or intended to evade: equivocal 逃避的,闪烁 其词的
words.example: If there was an organizing principle in Attorney General Jeff Sessions’s testimony before the Senate Intelligence Committee on Tuesday—which was by turns dismissive, incoherent, evasive, and shameful—it was that certain people are owed deference, and certain others are not.

words.meaning: n. the act of giving money, food, or other kinks of help to people who are poor, sick, etc. 慈善
words.example: The Willard T. C. Johnson Foundation had given $250,000, and the Charity Fight Night Foundation, a celebrity charity that raises money for the Muhammad Ali Parkinson Center and other causes, had donated $150,000.

words.meaning: (1)n. the act of making fun of someone or something in a cruel or harsh way 嘲弄 (2)v. to laugh at and make jokes about in a cruel or harsh way 嘲笑
words.example: As of late Saturday, the plans for the protest had drawn ridicule from Kellyanne Conway, a trusted Trump adviser.

words.meaning: v. to shrink away or crouch 畏缩
v. to get rid of 丢弃
words.example: If anybody could isolate the truly astounding aspects of Melo’s game and convince him to jettison, or at least minimize, the rest, the Zen master could.

words.meaning: n. a person who does not behave the way most people behave 不守成规者
words.example: He plays Mr. Keating, a nonconformist teacher at an all-boys school, the part made famous by Robin Williams.

words.meaning: (1)adj. fashioned from whatever is immediately available 临 时的 (2)adj. formed or used for a special purpose 特别的
words.example: At an ad hoc meeting of the Darfur People’s Association, immigration lawyers help men understand their cases.

words.meaning: n. a quick and cheerful readiness to do something 乐意
words.example: The Republican senators have submitted their blue slips with alacrity.

words.meaning: adj. very old or old-fashioned 过时的
words.example: Thomas basically embraced this antediluvian view of the Constitution, writing, “I am aware of no cases prior to the New Deal that characterized the power flowing from the Commerce Clause as sweepingly as does our substantial effects test.”

words.meaning: adj. causing unhappiness or sad feelings 枯燥乏味的
words.example: Her father lived for a year so dreary and relentlessly full of pain that she was forced to wish him dead.

words.meaning: (1)n. morally good behavior or character 美德 (2)n. a commendable quality or courage 优点
words.example: Traditionally, handwriting was elevated to the realm of the virtues. Accordingly, every technology that made writing easier had to be treated with contempt.

words.meaning: n. the state of being very annoyed or upset 恼怒
words.example: Her forehands were suddenly sailing long, her double faults were mounting, and her body language was projecting exasperation and unease.

words.meaning: n. the beginning of something 开始
words.example: Later, after the onset of puberty, the Rock spent time doing various strength-training exercises and acquired more muscle than he’d had as a baby.

words.meaning: (1)adj. very dirty 肮脏的 (2)adj. very bad or dishonest 卑 鄙的,不诚实的
words.example: Trump said that, in order to deny Bill Clinton the Presidency, he was prepared to scrutinize “every nook and cranny of Clinton’s sordid personal life.”

words.meaning: adj. tending or serving to overwhelm 势不可挡的,压倒一 切的
adj. mandatory, enforced 强迫的
adj. odd or unpredictable in their appearance, character, or behavior. 奇怪的
words.example: The reissue of Moore’s original work will shift the perception of Moore from a quirky but somewhat cold formalist to a restlessly imaginative experimenter.

words.meaning: (1)adj. unsure or uncertain 不确定的 (2)adj. causing doubt, uncertainty, or suspicion 可疑的
words.example: Even Chinese commentators who are utterly skeptical of the President have pointed to Ivanka as the most respectable of an otherwise dubious cohort.

words.meaning: (1)v. to cause to sleep or rest 使平静 (2)v. to cause to feel safe and relaxed instead of careful and alert 蒙蔽 (3)n. a brief time when an action or activity stops 间歇
words.example: Its noises—slight splashes, fish thrashing on the bottom of a boat—threatened, several times, to lull me to sleep.

words.meaning: adj. extremely important and necessary 不可或缺的
words.example: The jazz guitarist and banjoist Danny Barker is said to have appeared on more than a thousand recordings. He’s also responsible for one of the most indispensable accounts of the music’s early days.

words.meaning: n. a concealed or unexpected difficulty or obstacle 障碍
words.example: Donald J. Trump’s foreign trip hit a snag on Tuesday, when the remaining countries on his itinerary announced that they would rather “wait a month” and meet with the next President instead.

words.meaning: v. to give energy or excitement to 使...活跃
words.example: Upon waking, Vongerichten and Cole each drink water with lemon, and crave foods that will sustain and energize them throughout the day.

words.meaning: adj. not stopping or growing weaker 不停的
words.example: His pictures possess no unity of composition, only unremitting energy. Everywhere your eye goes, it finds things to engage it.

words.meaning: adj. full of mystery and difficult to understand 神秘难懂的
words.example: Ashbery’s poetry is invariably described as difficult and enigmatic; in that sense, the man was distant from his writing, since, in person, he was anything but an enigma.

words.meaning: (1)adj. of, belonging to, or seeming to come from an early time in the very ancient past 原始的 (2)adj. very simple and basic 初级的,简陋的
words.example: Because the single most striking thing about his matchlessly strange first week is how primitive, atavistic, and uncomplicatedly brutal Trump’s brand of authoritarianism is turning out to be.

words.meaning: adj. impossible to understand 费解的
words.example: Obama’s inaction is as incomprehensible as it is reprehensible, especially considering that the Obama Administration had several months to respond before the election, and that it was plain that Russian hacking was undermining the democratic process.

words.meaning: (1)v. to cause to happen or be created 使产生 (2)v. to cause someone to have (a feeling or emotion) 激起,引起
words.example: A theme of the night, inspired by life in 2017, was the John Cale lyric “Fear is a man’s best friend.”

words.meaning: (1)v. to say or declare openly 公开宣称 (2)v. to believe in (a particular religion) 信奉
words.example: The reason that the Clinton and the Trump campaigns are embracing negativity, a strategy that so many voters profess to detest, is somewhat paradoxical: it works.

words.meaning: adj. restricted or peculiar to a locality or region 地方性的
words.example: China is so big and so hungry that its appetites could well doom numerous species at once, from the armored pangolin to the Knysna sea horse, a monogamous fish endemic to South African estuaries.

words.meaning: v. to make safe or secure 保卫
words.example: At its head sat a barely literate man, rude and reckless, a rascally treasure hunter installed by a beleaguered group of purists eager to safeguard their privileges and padlock their ranks.

words.meaning: adj. no longer useful or acceptable 过时的
words.example: The reasons that musicians record on outdated, archaic, and less flexible equipment are, in some sense, surprising.

words.meaning: (1)v. to make dark or to become dark or darker in color 使 变暗 (2)v. to make less happy or to become less happy 使忧郁
words.example: Soon enough, the skies darken and the rain comes down in sheets.

words.meaning: (1)v. to speak ill of 贬低 (2)v. to take away 减损
words.example: But that lapse in judgment on Romney’s part doesn’t detract from the power of his statement on Friday, or from the reality that Trump’s Presidency is in crisis, his very legitimacy in question.

words.meaning: (1)v. to drink alcohol 饮酒 (2)v. to receive into the mind and retain 吸收,接受
words.example: As unappealing as a leafy Tostitos lime cocktail with hints of S.P.F. 50 may sound, this Bar Tabber managed to imbibe some winning drinks in the line of duty in 2015.

words.meaning: adj. complete in every detail 完美的
words.example: The comic actor who directs himself with a consummate mastery of technique—there’s a noble tradition at work, and when Lewis planned to direct he had that tradition, with Charlie Chaplin at the head of it, in mind.

words.meaning: adj. getting what is wanted in a clever and often deceptive way 狡猾的
words.example: Moral: beware the devious, the slippery, the cunning Canadian.

words.meaning: (1)adj. not knowing or caring about people and events in other places 狭隘的 (2)adj. of, relating to, or coming from a province 省的
words.example: The irony of digital networking is that it can produce more provincialism than did the geographical confinement it supposedly transcended.

words.meaning: adj. not lasting forever 短暂的
words.example: His game-changing shows remind us that ours is an impermanent and fragile world.

words.meaning: n. a combination or mixture of things 融合
words.example: Depending on the quality of your introduction to sesame-crusted tuna or similar sanitized novelties at trendier- than-thou enclaves, your opinion of Asian fusion may occupy some intersection of tortured and tacky.

words.meaning: v. to lessen the intensity of 减轻,缓和
adj. difficult to handle, control, or deal with because of being large, heavy, or complex 笨重的,难操作的
words.example: In her column “Ask Polly,” Heather Havrilesky attempts to address her readers’ most unwieldy questions about modern life.

words.meaning: adj. 包罗万象的
words.example: For a left-wing protest politician, it is fine, and perhaps even imperative, to have a single, all-encompassing world view.

words.meaning: adj. very important or noticeable 显著的,重要的
words.example: It is in this respect that Le Pen’s book seems most salient at the present, when women in several countries are playing crucial roles in promoting and sanitizing far-right populism.

words.meaning: (1)v. to have particular meaning or importance for someone 对某人有重要性 (2)v. to continue to produce a loud, clear, deep sound for a long time 共振 (3)v. to relate harmoniously 一致
words.example: I would like to stop reading about this disaffected sliver of the electorate and instead read about attempts to mobilize progressives and people of color around a platform that might even resonate with W.W.C. voters, wherever they live.

words.meaning: v. to catch 俘获
words.example: Korben wants to be the first filmmaker to capture physical, visual evidence of the metaphysical realm—and the ghosts that he wants to capture on film are his own.

words.meaning: v. to get ahead of 超过
words.example: Does Donald Trump outstrip the novelist’s imagination?

words.meaning: v. to get worse 衰退
words.example: Those beloved tales had begun to ebb from memory by the beginning of the twentieth century, as the Arctic gradually lost its political and cultural stakes.

words.meaning: v. to go through very carefully in order to find something useful or valuable 筛选
words.example: It was telling that Biden had to sift through nearly a half century of history to find a precedent for the current malaise among liberals and progressives, but the comparison was not entirely fitting.

words.meaning: n. the state of being old and in bad condition or poor health 衰老,老朽
words.example: But revulsion at the cronyism and the decrepitude of major-party politics can’t be what’s driving Jeb Bush and Mitt Romney, two eminent members of the establishment.

words.meaning: adj. causing unnecessary fear or anxiety that something unpleasant or dangerous is going to happen 危言耸听的
words.example: May accused Labour and the media of making alarmist claims, and argued weakly about the basic compromises necessary to insure care for an aging population.

words.meaning: (1)n. deliberate purposive planning 故意 (2)n. the process of planning how something will look, happen, be made 设计
words.example: We can make the decision to be different by design, and try some unconventional tactics to stand out from the herd.

words.meaning: (1)v. to fluctuate or veer between one point, position, or opinion and another 摇摆 (2)v. to exert a building or controlling influence on 影响
words.example: Can Obama’s hope sway the French election?

words.meaning: adj. done with or showing artistic skill 巧妙的
words.example: It’s an artful variant of the familiar nightmare—familiar, indeed, as a fairy tale—of the amnesiac waking to a jigsaw-puzzle world to be reassembled by heroic effort.

words.meaning: v. to make seem smaller or less important 不予重视
words.example: In previous White Houses, officials downplayed this sort of internal combat, insisting that everyone was united around the President’s agenda.

words.meaning: v. to keep in a position, job, etc. 保持
words.example: On Sunday, the people of France annoyingly retained their traditional right to claim intellectual superiority over Americans, as millions of French citizens paused to enjoy just how much smarter they were than their allies across the Atlantic.

words.meaning: adj. existing or continuing in the same way for a long time 持久的
words.example: Armenia is chess’s perennial overachiever, and Levon Aronian, its greatest player, is a swashbuckling throwback.

words.meaning: adj. 半成品的
words.example: Partly because of Lee’s skill at synthesizing emergent trends drawn from many fields of study, her theories appeared to present, with uncanny aptness, ideas already half-formulated in the minds of her contemporaries.

words.meaning: adj. inclined by nature to companionship with others of the same species 好交际的
words.example: At the Rock County Job Center, Matt took a test called JobFit. The test assessed his manual dexterity, math skills, and sociability, and concluded that he should become a database developer, a podiatrist, or a nurse.

words.meaning: adj. capable of being understood or appreciated 容易理解 的
words.example: According to the terms of the proposed draft order, every taxi in Los Angeles would have to become accessible via a mobile application similar to the ones used by Uber and Lyft.

words.meaning: adj. following one’s own capricious, wanton, or depraved inclinations 任性的
words.example: In her career as an editor, she pruned and shaped many a writer’s wayward prose into crisp lucidity.

words.meaning: adj. not done or experienced before 前所未有的
words.example: The Administration official said that Lynch and Justice Department officials are studying the situation, which he called unprecedented.

words.meaning: v. to stop developing, progressing, moving, etc. 停滞
words.example: It anatomized how the federal government was failing to enforce immigration laws, and how immigration was causing wages to stagnate and unemployment to persist.

words.meaning: n. passive assent or agreement without protest 默许
words.example: It is likely that these numbers are even higher in rural areas where few studies are conducted, few cases reported—either out of fear or in acquiescence to cultural realities.

words.meaning: v. to eliminate the interrelationship of 使...没有联系
words.example: These efforts might serve to buy some time while the economists and diplomats in the Administration could find a palatable way to decouple the dollar from gold.

words.meaning: n. small bumps on your skin that are caused by cold, fear, or a sudden feeling of excitement 鸡皮疙瘩
words.example: My arms prickled with goose bumps, and the minute and a half of totality felt no longer than a head-spinning ten seconds.

words.meaning: adj. based on testing or experience 根据实证或经验的
words.example: This ballet of musical objects not only illustrates Aristotle’s notion of a fundamental form outside the empirical realm—as matter decays, the spirit persists—but also celebrates the sonic diversity of avant-garde composition.

words.meaning: (1)v. to stop from happening or to cause to happen at a later time 预先阻止 (2)v. to act before in order to prevent something 提前行动
words.example: To forestall further acts of terror, Bangladesh's government will have to face up to that grim new reality.

words.meaning: phrase. 达到,不辜负
words.example: The smallest human societies that we can identify, either among living groups or among the populations of prehistory, do not appear to live up to the image of primitive affluence that has become popular among anthropologists in the past twenty years.

words.meaning: adj. full of sadness or sorrow 悲伤的
words.example: Paintings, prints, and statuary of the extinct beast, a lugubrious mascot, lurk everywhere you look

words.meaning: adj. used to say that two or more things are very similar and can be compared to each other 相似的
words.example: The question is not whether C.E.O.s have salaries hundreds of times larger than their own but whether black people have salaries comparable to theirs.

words.meaning: v. to refuse to accept 拒绝
words.example: Even the album’s cover art explicitly channels the mind-set of teen-agers and twentysomethings who, liberated by the Internet, spurn labels and celebrate eccentricity.

words.meaning: adj. very complicated and difficult to understand 复杂难解 的
words.example: According to The New Yorker, the corporate structures of his various enterprises are so convoluted that it is difficult to assess the true extent of his holdings.

words.meaning: adj. tending to make people afraid or less confident 使人畏 惧的
words.example: The numbers are absolutely daunting — 52,000 overdose deaths in 2015; 33,000 of those approximately related to opioids. The numbers in 2016 are no better, and the numbers in 2017 are even worse than 2016.

words.meaning: adj. of or relating to money 金钱的
words.example: A more worthwhile question is whether Basquiat rates high in valuations of recent art apart from the pecuniary.

words.meaning: adj. having a mutual or reciprocal relation 相互联系的
words.example: At the time of his speech at Riverside Church, King had come to see war, poverty, and racism as interrelated; taking on one necessarily meant confronting the others.

words.meaning: v. to shorten in duration or extent 删减
words.example: Given the flood of information presented by the mass media, the only way for someone to keep abreast of the news is to rely on abridged accounts

words.meaning: v. to slow the movement, progress, or action of 阻碍
words.example: Obama made it clear that the kind of hacking ”that could hamper vote counting, affect the actual election process itself” was his most urgent concern.

words.meaning: v. to cause to be very interested in 使着迷
words.example: Ants fascinate me, with their matriarchy, industry, and powerful sense of smell, which they use to recognize the caste of other colony members.

words.meaning: (1)adj. full of or exuding emotion or spirit 热烈的 (2)adj. easily provoked 暴躁的
words.example: Cool, brutal, elegant, fiery, he established a kind of International Style in music, and propagated it in polemical writings and through institutional networks.

words.meaning: n. a feeling of being frustrated or annoyed because of failure or disappointment 失望
words.example: Part of the deal is that we still feign chagrin or incredulity (or both) when a pop star announces another reinvention.

words.meaning: v. to make rich or richer especially by the addition or increase of some desirable quality, attribute, or ingredient 使丰富
words.example: On this week’s Out Loud, Gopnik joins host Sasha Weiss, the literary editor of newyorker.com, for a stroll through SoHo and the Village to discuss how walking in the neighborhood has changed over time, and how walking can enrich the experience of urban life.

words.meaning: adj. happening or done very often 常出现的
words.example: Precipitation is not regular in the Atacama Desert west of Andes mountains, and water scarcity is compounded by problems with water appropriation, the existence of agro pastoralists is in jeopardy.

words.meaning: (1)adj. complete or total 完全的,不受限制的 (2)adj. not having the skills, knowledge, or experience needed to do a particular job or activity 无资格的
words.example: Kim Jong-un was a “totally unqualified person” who attained his position of power only through nepotism.

words.meaning: (1)v. to take a child of other parents legally as your own child 收养 (2)v. to begin to use or have 采用

words.meaning: n. a nervous or fearful feeling of uncertain agitation 不安
words.example: I followed Duckpenisgate with particular trepidation, since I was one of the co-investigators on the maligned study.

words.meaning: adj. 对自身不利的
adj. likely to change frequently without apparent for cogent reason 善变的
words.example: Leadership may be, by its nature, an anxious and inconstant idea. Like “status” or “alienation,” the word “leadership” points not toward a stable concept but toward a problem or affliction unique to modernity.

words.meaning: n. false information deliberately 故意的假情报
(1)adj. lacking confidence 缺乏自信的 (2)adj. very careful about acting or speaking 言行谨慎的
words.example: On the campaign trail, though, he was diffident and underpowered—except when the subject was Khan.

words.meaning: (1)adj. pleasing to the mind or senses 令人愉悦的 (2)adj. able to be accepted 合适的,可被接受的
v. to bring into consonance or accord 使一致
words.example: To me, this suffusion of language and light is like a chorus, because the scrolling words must harmonize with light, or push up against it.

words.meaning: adj. simple and plain 简朴的
words.example: Before there was Marie Kondo, who taught us the magic of tidiness, or Hillary Clinton,R who made a white pantsuit into a mantra, there was Sara Berman, whose frugal all-white wardrobe is the subject of a new exhibit at the Metropolitan Museum of Art.

words.meaning: v. to change to an opposite state or condition 转向,颠倒
words.example: Education Secretary Betsy DeVos is being dispatched to Pyongyang in what the White House is calling a high- stakes mission to reverse North Korea’s years of progress in math and science.

words.meaning: n. an angry and usually long speech or piece of writing that strongly criticizes someone or something 抨击
words.example: Before “Fresh off the Boat” premièred on TV, in 2015, Huang wrote a striking diatribe in New York magazine, accusing the sitcom’s producers of whitewashing his memoir in order to pander to mainstream audiences.

words.meaning: (1)adj. lacking dexterity or skill 笨拙的 (2)adj. lacking the right proportions, size, or harmony of parts 不合适的 (3)adj. causing embarrassment 尴尬的 (4)adj. not easy to handle or deal with 棘手的
words.example: Despite his awkward-looking posture, he managed to reach Mayweather with an irritating jab and, occasionally, a hard uppercut.

words.meaning: adj. penetrating only the easily or quickly perceived 肤浅的
words.example: The two succeeding episodes—on “History’s Worst Medical Advice” and “The Drug That Changed the World”—match the première in being rather shallow and arbitrary.

words.meaning: (1)adj. fully revealed or expressed without vagueness, implication, or ambiguity 直截了当的 (2)adj. fully developed or formulated 详述的
words.example: Sometimes the analysis is explicit and conscious, though in most cases it's subconscious and embedded in euphemism.

words.meaning: adj. very clever and able to make intelligent 精明的
words.example: Steidl prides himself on being a canny businessman: he has always wanted to make money, and funnels it back into the business when he does.

words.meaning: adj. capable of being easily led, taught, or controlled 驯服 的,温顺的
words.example: Only afterward does he realize the possibility that Flicka has inherited not just the speed and strength of the Albino but also the unruliness of his blood—that there is a chance that she might never become tractable.

words.meaning: v. to grow or develop quickly 迅速生长
words.example: Folding tables burgeon with cables, clipboards, and fast-food impedimenta.

words.meaning: adj. arrested in development or adjustment 过分迷恋的
words.example: It was opened, in March, by Felipe Mendez Candelas, an obsessive record collector who has lately been fixated on African dance music, tropical house, and disco.

words.meaning: (1)adj. not letting light through 不透明的 (2)adj. difficult to understand or explain 难懂的
words.example: The smoke thickened into an opaque black funnel. The air smelled like a tire shop.

words.meaning: adj. difficult to understand 难解的
words.example: In a file in Hahn’s papers at the Lilly Library, in Bloomington, Indiana, I’d found a square of white silk, covered from edge to edge with typewritten names, cryptic messages, and several lines of Japanese poetry.

words.meaning: v. to make confused or upset 使难堪
words.example: Like his name, the track is an enthusiastic but discomfiting homage that raises questions about appropriation and sincerity.

words.meaning: adj. causing feelings of sadness and sympathy 可怜的
words.example: The images out of Charlottesville, Virginia, are unsettling—but also pathetic, also exasperating—for their boring timelessness.

words.meaning: (1)n. the act of giving or delivering something to people 分 配 (2)n. the position, arrangement, or frequency of occurrence over an area or through a space or unit of time 分布
words.example: Relying on herd immunity is shortsighted, since over time fewer people will be infected and the virus can gain a new foothold. Indeed, the geographic distribution of Aedes aegypti, the mosquito species that transmits Zika, is expanding.

words.meaning: (1)v. to cause to happen quickly or suddenly 引发,加速 (2)n. a solid substance that is separated from al liquid especially by a chemical process 沉淀 (3)adj. happening very quickly or too quickly without enough thought or planning 冲动的,鲁莽的
words.example: Democrats staged an all-night session in which Senators Chuck Schumer, Elizabeth Warren, and others voiced their objections to DeVos’s ideology, and to the precipitate process of her confirmation.

words.meaning: adj. relating to having a strict and simple way of living that avoids physical pleasure 苦行的
words.example: Where Paterson is ascetic and gaunt, murmuring poems to himself, Neruda is corpulent and unabashed, declaiming to his disciples and eager to gorge on the sins of the flesh.

words.meaning: adj. too important and respected to be changed, criticized, etc. 神圣不可改变的
words.example: Most significant, the week after Trump signed his now unravelling travel ban, the Museum of Modern Art replaced seven works in its sacrosanct fifth-floor galleries.

words.meaning: v. to avoid especially because you do not think it is right, proper, etc. 避免
words.example: David uses his expertise to help people challenge parking tickets and reduce or even eschew fines when possible.

words.meaning: (1)v. to claim to be or do a particular thing when this claim may not be true (假意)声称 (2)n. the main or general meaning of something 意义
words.example: In its title and introduction, the book purports to be conversant with contemporary discourse in the field, but in its actual analysis of Wharton’s work, it is marked by a very old-fashioned approach.

words.meaning: (1)v. to make excessive claims for 吹嘘 (2)v. to sell too much or too many 过多出售
words.example: Enthusiasts, including researchers, perpetually oversell the microbiome.

words.meaning: v. to make perpetual or cause to last indefinitely 使...永存
words.example: The whole premise of baseball-umpire camp is to perpetuate naïve realism.

words.meaning: adj. free of encumbrance 无阻碍的
words.example: His client confessed that the novelty of being unencumbered had him lying awake nights.

words.meaning: n. the quality or state of being accurate and correct 精确性
words.example: Macho and minimalist alike, the sentences are thrilling still in their exactitude and audacity.

words.meaning: v. to become weaker 减弱
words.example: After the storm pounded the Texas coast, it crept inland and then stopped moving, as if mired in mud, and its torrential rains are not expected to abate for many days.

words.meaning: adj. precisely adapted to a particular situation, need, or circumstance 合适的
words.example: Debate rages on between proponents of corporal punishment and the death penalty and their detractors, though even the most rabid supporter agrees that punishments must be meet and the justice system evenhanded and thorough.

words.meaning: (1)n. an amount of money that is used for a special purpose 资金 (2)v. to provide money for 为...提供资金
words.example: Even though the source of funding has been determined to be a less significant cause of bias than other factors, industry support suffices, in the minds of many people, to vitiate the credibility of scientific work.

words.meaning: n. good-natured and usually witty and animated joking 打 趣
words.example: The atmosphere was at its most elated on Fridays, when a communal sense of liberation descended, so that proximity could lead to banter.

words.meaning: adj. acting as a mediator 调解的,中间的
n. a small amount 少量
words.example: This flawed structure was also based on the premise, a naïve one at best, that the United States had a national interest in moderating Israeli behavior and achieving a modicum of justice in the Middle East.

words.meaning: v. to incite to action or accelerated growth or development 激励
words.example: welder puts together part of a bicycle frame at the Detroit Bikes facility. The upstart bicycle manufacturer is hoping to spur a revival of a made-in-America industry in the city.

words.meaning: adj. producing money or wealth 获利丰厚的
words.example: In boxing, the right person doesn’t always win, and being hated is nearly as lucrative as being loved.

words.meaning: adj. too eager to help or obey someone important 谄媚的
words.example: Publicly, Pence has shown nothing but unconditional—at times even obsequious and worshipful—support for Trump. His private actions, however, suggest a more calculating and realistic mind-set.

words.meaning: adj. moving in an awkward or clumsy way 笨拙的,不优 雅的
words.example: We’ve come to expect elegance in Olympic victories, but Shaunae Miller’s gutsy, ungainly lunge across the finish line in the women’s four hundred metres reminded us that winning isn’t always pretty.

words.meaning: adj. relaxed and casual in style or manner 悠闲的
words.example: As Al Gore’s press secretary, in 2000, he led the double-black-diamond effort of making the Vice-President seem loose and easygoing on the campaign trail.

words.meaning: adj. having or showing sad thoughts and feelings about something that you want to have or do and especially about something that made you happy in the past 伤感 的,依依不舍的
words.example: Wistful memories and sweet moments intermingle as she recalls what she has lost and how she has learned to heal herself.

words.meaning: (1)v. to state or declare positively and often forcefully or aggressively 断言 (2)v. the demonstrate the existence of 宣称
words.example: The university tried to get him to submit a patent for the unreleased cultivars, in order to assert its right to make use of Shaw’s intimate knowledge of the plants.

words.meaning: adj. messy or untidy 不整洁的
words.example: “We try to keep that balance between unkempt and orderly,” says Rob Warren, the owner, surveying his domain, a scruffy, comfortable place with an exhaustive Beat Generation section just inside the front door.

words.meaning: (1)adj. not interesting 乏味的 (2)adj. too simple 幼稚的
words.example: Commercial novels, in general, whether they’re thrillers or romance or science fiction, employ language that is at best undistinguished and at worst characterized by a jejune mentality and a tendency to state the obvious.

words.meaning: adj. beyond what is needed 多余的
words.example: In the realm of Holocaust dramas, László Nemes’s first feature is ambitious and provocative but nearly superfluous.

words.meaning: adj. staying strong and in good condition over a long period of time 持久的
words.example: At once aggressive and defensive, punitive and protective, the Venetian ghetto proved to be a remarkably durable arrangement—it was abolished, under Napoleon, only with the fall of the Serenissima in 1797.

words.meaning: adj. growing quickly and in a way that is difficult to control 泛滥的,疯长的
words.example: The unexpected election of Donald Trump is said to owe debts to both niche extremism and rampant misinformation.

words.meaning: adj. characterized by steady, earnest, and energetic effort 勤奋的
words.example: Mary Garth, the quiet, diligent young woman whom many readers have seen as the alternative heroine of Eliot’s story, has been turned into Max Garth, a gay townie who resembles Mary in his humility and intelligence, if not in his appearance.

words.meaning: adj. very good 优秀的
words.example: Given the sterling reputation of the committees and the exhaustive nature of its investigation, it would be unreasonable to gainsay the committee’s conclusions at first glance.

words.meaning: (1)v. to prevent from happening 阻止 (2)v. to cause to decide not to do something 威慑
words.example: Through adamant protests and influence wielding, outspoken Greenwich natives were able to deter the UN from settling on their land.

words.meaning: v. to be thought about or worried about by (someone) very often or constantly 使全神贯注
words.example: If Harpo had been endowed with the power of speech, he might have touched on the subjects that urgently preoccupy McBurney, which are mystery, love, and the nature of time.

words.meaning: n. a very small difference in color, tone, meaning, etc. 细微 差别
words.example: Jim and I shared many qualities: an unending appetite, inhaling life to the full chorizo, finding hilarious and playful nuance in every breath and every moment, but I always was and remain the student.

words.meaning: (1)v. to cause confusion in 使...混乱 (2)n. a state of confusion or disorder 混乱
words.example: More broadly, the Administration’s policy in the region has evolved into a destabilizing muddle.

words.meaning: adj. refusing to do what other people want 固执的
words.example: Her photograph shows an aged and obdurate man with a tinge of uncertainty in his face.

words.meaning: adj. not kind or sympathetic toward other people 无情的
words.example: It’s almost pathological, his inability to distinguish his lovers from one another; and yet he doesn’t strike me as a bad guy, or even an unfeeling one.

words.meaning: v. to make more violent, bitter, or severe 使恶化
words.example: The cheapest PSAPS, some of which sell for less than fifty dollars, are notoriously junky and may even exacerbate hearing loss by indiscriminately amplifying harmful sounds.

words.meaning: n. the act of causing two people or groups to become friendly again after an argument or disagreement 和解
words.example: Is reconciliation possible after the election?

words.meaning: adj. wildly extravagant 挥霍的
words.example: Nonetheless, I had a lot more fun with “Hudson Hawk” than I ever did with “Die Hard” or “The Terminator,” because it offers, from beginning to end, such a prodigious, even profligate, display of imagination.

words.meaning: n. the state of having identical cumulative distribution function or values 同质
words.example: The homogeneity amounted to a defense of identity.

words.meaning: v. to complain about peevishly 抱怨
words.example: With all the non-stop all-night crying, will the neighbors crab, or will they be carted away under cover of darkness, never to be heard from again?

words.meaning: (1)adj. very interesting 激发兴趣的 (2)adj. capable of causing someone to believe or agree 令人信服的 (3)adj. strong and forceful 强大的
words.example: The British-Ghanaian artist creates compelling character studies of people who don’t exist, reflecting her twin talents as a writer and a painter.

words.meaning: (1)adj. dirty and in bad condition form being neglected 肮 脏的 (2)adj. dishonest or immoral 不诚实的,邪恶的
words.example: During the recent James Comey hearings, Pratt again invoked the genre’s sleazy tactics: E“The producers gotta give this cast some more drinks.”

words.meaning: n. insulting or contemptuous action or speech 嘲笑
words.example: The decision was met with mockery from those interested in government transparency and with insinuations, from Republican quarters, that it was a symptom of the Obama Administration’s unwillingness to openly call acts like this works of Islamic terrorism.

words.meaning: adj. not having or showing a desire to be noticed, praised, etc. 谦逊的
words.example: Much has been written about the unassuming smile that never leaves that tiny head.

words.meaning: adj. hard to understand 深奥难懂的
words.example: They continue to write in this way, deliberately obscure and esoteric, because it is a shortcut to being mysterious.

words.meaning: v. to make a loud, high-pitched cry 尖叫
words.example: She shrieked as I imagined an eagle would shriek.

words.meaning: adj. showing dislike or opposition 敌对的
words.example: If it were done by a Northerner, it will have an antagonistic effect,” she wrote. More precisely, it might have jeopardized support for the New Deal among members of Congress from the former Confederate states.

words.meaning: (1)adj. believable or realistic 真实的 (2)adj. superficially fair, reasonable, or valuable but often specious 看似真实 (但实际虚假)
words.example: If there is no true news, false news can be made very plausible.

words.meaning: v. to put or keep apart 割断,断绝
words.example: The three joined a growing list of nonprofits that have severed ties with the exclusive, Trump-owned resort.

words.meaning: adj. very cruel 残酷的
words.example: “I might be accused of being heartless for saying this, but this person should be thrown out on the street,” he said.

words.meaning: adj. of or relating to a citizen, a city, citizenship, or community affairs 公民的
words.example: It is a moment of indeterminate morality, one in which the centrifugal forces of contempt, resentment, and racial superiority are pitted against the ideal of common humanity and the possibility of a civic society.

words.meaning: v. to divide into separate sections 分开
words.example: It allows her, for the first time, to see the truth of her splintered identity — to realize that her ability to act and lie and compartmentalize, to show different faces to the world, calls her very being into question.

words.meaning: v. to criticize very harshly 严厉批评
words.example: In his Sunday newsletter, the journalist Mike Allen asked if anyone had noticed “the crude, discriminating language and signs used by some to lambaste a president they condemn as crude and discriminating.

words.meaning: v. to choose not to do 克制
words.example: She was never precipitate; she was nothing if not discreet, so she forbore for the present to declare her passion.

words.meaning: adj. tedious from familiarity 陈旧的,厌倦的
words.example: He travelled with a collection of old newspapers and stale crackers; he liked to wear several layers of clothing.

words.meaning: (1)v. to turn away or aside 转移 (2)v. to prevent from happening 防止
words.example: When Congress returns in September the House will have just 12 legislative days to raise the federal borrowing limit to avoid default — and the same amount of time to approve a spending deal to avert a government shutdown.

words.meaning: adj. of a commonplace or repetitious character 平凡乏味的
words.example: The bombing at Atatürk International Airport was the sixth major terrorist attack in Turkey this year. The response to these events has become almost routine.

words.meaning: n. a charitable act or gift 慈善,善行
words.example: The philosophers’ God was not necessarily identical to the God of Christianity, but he had some reassuringly familiar attributes, such as beneficence and providential oversight of the world.

words.meaning: adj. unusual or surprising: likely to be noticed 显著而惊讶 的
words.example: Vang’s “Afterland” and Matthews’s “Simulacra” are remarkable, virtuosic collections from young poets.

words.meaning: adj. happening at the correct or most useful time 适时的
words.example: With her eerily timely “Indecent,” Paula Vogel unsettles American theatre again.

words.meaning: v. remodel, refashion 改写
words.example: Anthropologist Jane Goodall was stalwart in her determination to anthropomorphize the animals she observed with such empathy, and so resisted her editors’ attempt to recast her descriptions in more dispassionate language.

words.meaning: adj. not lasting long 短暂的
words.example: In extremely unfortunate cases, retrograde and anterograde amnesia can occur in the same individual, who is then said to suffer from “transient global amnesia,” a condition that is, thankfully, temporary.

words.meaning: (1)adj. working closely and well together 连贯的 (2)adj. able to talk or express yourself in a clear way that can be easily understood 条理清楚的
words.example: His inability to maintain, on any issue, a consistent or even coherent position has undercut his ability to inspire loyalty and respect on Capitol Hill.

words.meaning: v. to see beforehand 预见
words.example: The way this institution operates against its own people provides a preview of the future that both American and Israeli officials will now foresee for Palestinians in the occupied territories.

words.meaning: v. to support with evidence or authority 证实
words.example: According to the newspaper Welt am Sonntag, the German Embassy in Niger sent a cable to Berlin corroborating these weekly executions, and comparing the conditions in Libya’s migrant connection houses to those of Nazi concentration camps.

words.meaning: n. ideas or guesses about something that is not known 推 测
words.example: Discrepancies in ISIS’s claim of responsibility for the Manchester bombing have spurred speculation about the organization’s command of foreign operations.

words.meaning: n. to form an opinion or to make an estimate about something from known facts 推断
words.example: Although not stated explicitly in the story, we can extrapolate that to acquire as much knowledge as she can is a way for her to exert some control on a world that would otherwise only worry and confuse her.

words.meaning: v. to place in a very strong position that cannot easily be changed 使...牢固,确立
words.example: You might see this as a problem, but turning to Big Data-driven algorithms to fix it will only further entrench our reliance on code to tell us what is important about the world—which is what led to the problem in the first place.

words.meaning: v. to reduce or cause to contract 使减少
words.example: Jeff Zucker has gotten telephone calls of bitter complaint from Jared Kushner about the coverage of his father-in- law, but if the performance of Jake Tapper and others there is any indication, the attempt to intimidate CNN has not deflated any spirits.

words.meaning: (1)adj. marked by simplicity, frugality, or avoidance of luxury and comfort 简朴的 (2)n. a person of great courage and self-discipline 勇敢守纪律的人
words.example: I grew up as a bike rider in Manhattan, and I also worked as a bike messenger, where I absorbed the spartan, libertarian, every-man-for-himself ethos.

words.meaning: adj. having the same connotations, implications, or reference 同义的
words.example: Tim Ho Wan, long synonymous with premier dim sum, was anointed with the honor in 2009 in its native Hong Kong.

words.meaning: v. to make nervous 使紧张,使不安
words.example: The occasional mix-ups rattle candidates on the trail despite the presence of political strategist’s plotting every event with the tactical precision of military commanders.

words.meaning: adj. very small or unimportant 微不足道的,不重要的
words.example: Earth’s orbit is essentially circular, so the effect on our climate is negligible.

words.meaning: adj. emphasizing that it is better or greater than anything else of its kind 无可比拟的
words.example: Johan Cruyff’s ability to read the game was unsurpassed, but it wasn’t just his solo skills that set him apart.

words.meaning: adj. showing people and things in a way that resembles how they really look 比喻的,象征的
words.example: The artist has spent her career using abstract and figurative images to tell enigmatic stories. But a recent work has made her an incendiary figure.

words.meaning: (1)adj. full of anger and disagreement 易怒的 (2)adj. hard to manage or control 难对付的
words.example: Even in a fractious era, the filmmaker still believes that his documentaries can bring every viewer in.

words.meaning: adj. very small in amount or number 缺乏的
words.example: As water and food become increasingly scarce, neighbor will turn against neighbor.

words.meaning: phrase. 与...矛盾
words.example: The film falsely portrays President Lyndon B. Johnson as being at odds with Martin Luther King Jr. and even using the FBI to discredit him, as only reluctantly behind the Voting Rights Act of 1965 and as opposed to the Selma march itself.
